name: Bento Box
summary: A modular UI style built from varied content tiles, clear grouping, compact hierarchy, and repeatable grid logic.

palette: neutral surfaces with controlled accent tiles
typography: clean sans-serif with compact card hierarchy
surfaces: contained cards, panels, and tile groups
effects: subtle border, soft shadow, or flat separation
layout: asymmetric tile grid with clear grouping
imagery: product UI crops, icons, charts, and compact media tiles

notes: Avoid making every section a tile grid.
Match tile weight to content priority.
Use interaction to expose secondary detail.

avoid: Avoid overly playful tone for serious workflows.

recommendations: Use neutral base surfaces with a small accent system.
avoid: Avoid too many equally loud card colors.

recommendations: Use compact hierarchy with clear card titles.
Use simple paired icons for card scanning.
avoid: Avoid illustration clutter inside every tile.

recommendations: Use compact buttons and clear card-level actions.
avoid: Avoid multiple competing CTAs per tile.

recommendations: Define tile sizes, padding, radius, border, and media rules.

avoid: Avoid card-like nav containers unless needed.

recommendations: Use tile variants for metric, feature, media, proof, and CTA cards.
Use stable modules and compact summaries.
avoid: Avoid unpredictable masonry for operational data.

recommendations: Lift tile, reveal secondary action, or strengthen border.
Use shallow card elevation or border separation.
avoid: Avoid deep shadows on every tile.
Avoid toast-only feedback for card actions.

accessibilityRisks.risk: Dense card grids can hide reading order and action priority.
accessibilityRisks.mitigation: Preserve logical DOM order, clear headings, and one primary action per tile.

css: Use grid-template areas or stable column spans for tile hierarchy.
do: Give each tile a clear content job.
avoid: Do not use bento structure as generic decoration.
